finding: Etiology: continued growth of residual benign rests of cartilage displaced from the growth plate

differentials.name: Malignant degeneration in long-bone enchondromas in 15-20%
differentials.raw: Malignant degeneration in long-bone enchondromas in 15-20% (severe new pain in an adult patient, interval growth at imaging, loss of marginal definition, cortical disruption, local periosteal reaction)

differentials.name: Epidermoid inclusion cyst
differentials.raw: Epidermoid inclusion cyst (phalangeal tuft, history of trauma, more lucent)

differentials.name: Unicameral bone cyst
differentials.raw: Unicameral bone cyst (rare in hands, more radiolucent)

differentials.name: Giant cell tumor of tendon sheath
differentials.raw: Giant cell tumor of tendon sheath (commonly erodes bone, soft-tissue mass outside bone)

differentials.name: Fibrous dysplasia
differentials.raw: Fibrous dysplasia (rare in hand, mostly polyostotic)

differentials.name: Chondrosarcoma
differentials.raw: Chondrosarcoma (exceedingly rare in phalanges, metacarpals, metatarsals)

context: Age:   10-30 years; M÷F = 1÷1 Histo:   lobules of pure hyaline cartilage Location:  (usually solitary; multiple = enchondromatosis) (a)  in 40% small tubular bones of hand (most frequent tumor here), distal + mid aspects of metacarpals, proximal / middle phalanges ◊  Most common benign tumor of the hand! (b)  proximal femur, proximal humerus, tibia, radius, ulna, foot, rib (3%) Site:   central within medullary canal + metaphyseal; epiphysis only affected after closure of growth plate √  oval / round area of geographic destruction with lobulated contour + fine marginal line √  chondroid matrix:
